steps.title: Signal a surprising result
steps.content: The first sentence describes a crude method: a cardboard cutout and string. The second sentence says the method still produced a close approximation, so the transition must show contrast despite the rough technique.

steps.title: Choose the contrast transition
steps.content: The matching transition means nevertheless. Choice B signals similarity, Choice C signals restatement, and Choice D suggests the outcome was fitting or expected; none captures the surprising accuracy. The answer is A.
